basic_details.job_title: Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online
basic_details.organization_name: Indian Navy - Directorate of Manpower Planning & Recruitment, Naval Headquarters, New Delhi
basic_details.total_vacancies: To be decided based on service requirement
basic_details.job_location: All India (Transferable; posting at any Indian Navy location, JV or subsidiary)
basic_details.short_information: The Directorate of Manpower Planning & Recruitment, Naval Headquarters, New Delhi has released the Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) recruitment for 01/2027 and 02/2027 batches under the Agnipath Scheme for unmarried Indian male candidates with AICTE-recognised 3-year diplomas in engineering/electrical trades. Key facts:

application_fee.all_candidates_stage_i_inet_fee: ₹550/- + 18% GST (Total as applicable); payable online only
application_fee.stage_ii_process_fee: No separate fee for Stage II
application_fee.payment_mode: Online only - Net Banking / Visa / Master / RuPay Credit Card / Debit Card / UPI
application_fee.sponsor_candidates_ncc_c_certi_fee: Exempted from Stage I - INET; directly issued call-up for Stage II based on eligibility
application_fee.details: Note: Fee once paid shall not be refunded under any circumstances. Candidates found to have registered multiple times will be rejected and debarred; fee reimbursement will not be carried out in such cases. In case of unsuccessful payment (money deducted but admit card not generated), wait 7 working days for automatic refund.

age_limit.age_details: ₹30,000/month (Year 1) to ₹40,000/month (Year 4); Seva Nidhi corpus ₹10.04 Lakhs on completion
age_limit.details: Note: There are no age relaxations for Ex-Servicemen status for Agniveer enrolment.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfil all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date. Only unmarried Indian male candidates are eligible to apply. All qualifications must be from AICTE-recognised Polytechnic Institutes. | Agniveer (Apprentice) - Engineering & Electrical Streams | Criteria Details Essential Educational Qualification Passed Matriculation Examination with minimum 50% marks in aggregate from Boards of School Education recognised by Ministry of Education, Govt. of India AND completed a 3-year Diploma course from an AICTE-recognised Polytechnic Institute with minimum 50% marks in aggregate in any of the qualifying diploma trades (Engineering or Electrical as applicable). Marital Status Only unmarried male candidates are eligible. Candidates must give a certificate of being 'unmarried' at the time of enrolment. Agniveers will not be permitted to marry during their entire 4-year tenure. A candidate will be dismissed from service if he marries during tenure or is found to be already married despite giving an unmarried certificate. Nationality Indian male citizens only. Preferential Proficiency in sports, swimming, and extra-curricular activities is desirable.

vacancy_details.Vacancies: To be notified based on service requirement

vacancy_details.Stream: Refrigeration and Air Conditioning Repair and Maintenance
Instrumentation, Electronics Instrumentation and Control Engineering

vacancy_details.Vacancies: To be notified based on service requirement

Only unmarried male candidates are eligible. Candidates must give a certificate of being 'unmarried' at the time of enrolment. Agniveers will not be permitted to marry during their entire 4-year tenure. A candidate will be dismissed from service if he marries during tenure or is found to be already married despite giving an unmarried certificate.
Proficiency in sports, swimming, and extra-curricular activities is desirable.

discipline_wise_breakdown.details: Passed Matriculation Examination with minimum 50% marks in aggregate from Boards of School Education recognised by Ministry of Education, Govt. of India AND completed a 3-year Diploma course from an AICTE-recognised Polytechnic Institute with minimum 50% marks in aggregate in any of the qualifying diploma trades (Engineering or Electrical as applicable).

Only unmarried male candidates are eligible. Candidates must give a certificate of being 'unmarried' at the time of enrolment. Agniveers will not be permitted to marry during their entire 4-year tenure. A candidate will be dismissed from service if he marries during tenure or is found to be already married despite giving an unmarried certificate.

salary_details.details: Total Seva Nidhi on completion of 4-year tenure: ₹10.04 Lakhs (Agniveer's contribution + matching GoI contribution). Additional benefits include: | Risk, hardship, dress and travel allowances Non-contributory Life Insurance Cover of ₹48 Lakh for the duration of engagement Death Compensation: One-time ex-gratia of ₹44 Lakh (attributable to service) in addition to insurance cover Disability Compensation: One-time ex-gratia of ₹44 / ₹25 / ₹15 Lakh based on 100% / 75% / 50% disability 30 days leave per year; sick leave as per medical advice Medical facilities at service hospitals and CSD provisions No gratuity or pensionary benefits applicable

selection_process: Parameter Details
Mode Computer-based
Total Questions 100 questions of 1 mark each
Language Bilingual - Hindi & English
Type Objective / Multiple Choice
Sections English & General Awareness, Science, Mathematics, and Diploma (Trade Specific)
Duration 1 Hour
Negative Marking 0.25 marks deducted per wrong answer
Passing Criteria Must pass in all sections as well as in aggregate. Indian Navy reserves the right to determine pass marks in each section and in aggregate.
Centre Allocation At the discretion of the Indian Navy; state preference to be indicated by candidate
Gender 1.6 Km Run Squats (Uthak Baithak) Push-ups Bent Knee Sit-ups
Male 06 min 30 sec 20 15 15
Passing Criteria Must pass in all sections as well as in aggregate
Stage 1 - INET (Indian Navy Entrance Test)
Separate INET exams will be conducted for Electrical and Engineering entries. INET 2/2026 will be conducted in August 2026 with SSC as Exam Partner.
Stage 2 - Physical Fitness Test (PFT)
Qualifying in PFT is mandatory for selection. Candidates undergoing PFT do so at their own risk.
Stage 2 - Written Examination
Written exam is conducted only for candidates who qualify PFT. Two separate exams are conducted for Engineering and Electrical streams.
Stage 2 - Recruitment Medical Examination

Conducted for all candidates who qualify PFT. Candidates found medically unfit may appeal within 05 days at a designated Military Hospital allocated by the Indian Navy. Medical fitness certificates from hospitals other than designated Military Hospitals will not be considered. No second appeal is permissible.
Stage 3 - Pre-Enrolment Medical at INS Chilka
Pre-enrolment medical formalities of all selected candidates will be conducted at INS Chilka
Odisha. Induction is subject to qualifying in Pre-Enrolment Medical Examination. Candidates not qualifying will be rejected.
how_to_apply: Candidates must apply online only at https://joinindiannavy.gov.in. No other mode of application will be accepted. Applications can also be uploaded through Common Service Centres (CSC) across the country at a fee of ₹60 + GST (optional facility).
Steps to Apply Online:
Visit https://joinindiannavy.gov.in → Click on "Register" → Register as AVR (Apprentice).
After successful registration, click on "Login" → AVR (Apprentice) → INET 2/26 → Proceed to apply.
Fill in all details carefully. Incorrect declaration of information identified at any stage will result in cancellation of candidature.

Upload a recent passport-size colour photograph (taken not before May 2026), size 10 KB to 50 KB; front portrait in light background without head gear (except Sikhs). Date of photograph must be clearly written at the bottom.
A live photograph will be captured through the webcam during application form filling.
Pay the examination fee of ₹550/- + 18% GST online via net banking / Credit Card / Debit Card / UPI.
Give state preference for allocation of INET exam centre. Choice of centre cannot be changed once selected or allotted by the Indian Navy.
Candidates will be provided a post-closing correction window. An additional correction window will be activated post INET exam for 2 days.
Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded:
Matriculation Certificate and Marksheet (proof of date of birth and educational qualification)
Diploma Certificate and Consolidated Marksheet from AICTE-recognised Polytechnic Institute
Domicile Certificate
NCC 'C' Certificate (if applicable) - to be verified through DG NCC
Sponsorship Certificate (if applicable) - to be verified through CABS
Recent passport-size colour photograph (taken not before May 2026)
Documents to be Produced at INS Chilka at the time of Induction:
All original certificates as submitted/uploaded during online application
Self-attested copies of all certificates
Original NCC 'C' Certificate / Sponsorship Certificate (if held)
Police Verification Form duly verified by the Superintendent of Police of respective jurisdiction (from place of domicile or place of residence)
Call-up letter / Admit Card
Apply Online at: https://joinindiannavy.gov.in → Register → AVR (Apprentice) → INET 2/26

Call-up Letters cum Admit Card for Stage I - INET 2/2026 will be available for download from https://joinindiannavy.gov.in approximately one week before the online examination. No call-up letter will be sent by post. Only Electronic mode of communication will be used for contacting candidates.

content_sections.heading: What is Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026?
content_sections.text: The Directorate of Manpower Planning & Recruitment, Naval Headquarters, New Delhi has released the Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) recruitment for 01/2027 and 02/2027 batches under the Agnipath Scheme for unmarried Indian male candidates with AICTE-recognised 3-year diplomas in engineering/electrical trades. content_sections.heading: Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online
content_sections.text: The Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 online application process has officially commenced for candidates seeking enrollment in the 01/2027 and 02/2027 Batches. Eligible applicants can submit their application forms through the official Indian Navy recruitment portal during the prescribed registration period. Candidates are advised to complete the application process well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues. The Application Window (Stage I – INET 2/2026) opens on 05 June 2026 and will remain active until 05 July 2026 (05:00 PM). After the application period ends, the Indian Navy will provide a Correction Window on 06 July 2026, allowing candidates to rectify permissible errors in their submitted application forms. Applicants should carefully verify all personal, educational, and communication details before final submission. To apply online, candidates must first register on the official Indian Navy recruitment website using a valid email ID and mobile number. After registration, they need to fill in the online application form, upload the required documents, and submit the form successfully. It is recommended to keep scanned copies of the photograph, signature, educational certificates, and identity proof ready before starting the application process. Link 1: Apply Online for Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 Link 2: Apply Online for Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026

content_sections.text: Stage 1 - INET (Indian Navy Entrance Test) Separate INET exams will be conducted for Electrical and Engineering entries. INET 2/2026 will be conducted in August 2026 with SSC as Exam Partner. Stage 2 - Physical Fitness Test (PFT) Qualifying in PFT is mandatory for selection. Candidates undergoing PFT do so at their own risk. Stage 2 - Written Examination Written exam is conducted only for candidates who qualify PFT. Two separate exams are conducted for Engineering and Electrical streams. Stage 2 - Recruitment Medical Examination Conducted for all candidates who qualify PFT. Candidates found medically unfit may appeal within 05 days at a designated Military Hospital allocated by the Indian Navy. Medical fitness certificates from hospitals other than designated Military Hospitals will not be considered. No second appeal is permissible. Stage 3 - Pre-Enrolment Medical at INS Chilka Pre-enrolment medical formalities of all selected candidates will be conducted at INS Chilka, Odisha. Induction is subject to qualifying in Pre-Enrolment Medical Examination. Candidates not qualifying will be rejected.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates must apply online only at https://joinindiannavy.gov.in. No other mode of application will be accepted. Applications can also be uploaded through Common Service Centres (CSC) across the country at a fee of ₹60 + GST (optional facility). Steps to Apply Online: Visit https://joinindiannavy.gov.in → Click on "Register" → Register as AVR (Apprentice). After successful registration, click on "Login" → AVR (Apprentice) → INET 2/26 → Proceed to apply. Fill in all details carefully. Incorrect declaration of information identified at any stage will result in cancellation of candidature. Upload a recent passport-size colour photograph (taken not before May 2026), size 10 KB to 50 KB; front portrait in light background without head gear (except Sikhs). Date of photograph must be clearly written at the bottom. A live photograph will be captured through the webcam during application form filling. Pay the examination fee of ₹550/- + 18% GST online via net banking / Credit Card / Debit Card / UPI. Give state preference for allocation of INET exam centre. Choice of centre cannot be changed once selected or allotted by the Indian Navy. Candidates will be provided a post-closing correction window. An additional correction window will be activated post INET exam for 2 days. Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded: Matriculation Certificate and Marksheet (proof of date of birth and educational qualification) Diploma Certificate and Consolidated Marksheet from AICTE-recognised Polytechnic Institute Domicile Certificate NCC 'C' Certificate (if applicable) - to be verified through DG NCC Sponsorship Certificate (if applicable) - to be verified through CABS Recent passport-size colour photograph (taken not before May 2026) Documents to be Produced at INS Chilka at the time of Induction: All original certificates as submitted/uploaded during online application Self-attested copies of all certificates Original NCC 'C' Certificate / Sponsorship Certificate (if held) Police Verification Form duly verified by the Superintendent of Police of respective jurisdiction (from place of domicile or place of residence) Call-up letter / Admit Card Apply Online at: https://joinindiannavy.gov.in → Register → AVR (Apprentice) → INET 2/26 Call-up Letters cum Admit Card for Stage I - INET 2/2026 will be available for download from https://joinindiannavy.gov.in approximately one week before the online examination. No call-up letter will be sent by post. Only Electronic mode of communication will be used for contacting candidates.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for Agniveer (Apprentice) 01/2027 and 02/2027 Batch is 05 July 2026 (17:00 hrs). The correction window closes on 06 July 2026. Candidates are advised to apply well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues.

faq.question: Q2. Can female candidates apply for Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) 2026?
faq.answer: No. Only unmarried male candidates are eligible to apply for Agniveer (Apprentice) 01/2027 and 02/2027 Batch. Female candidates are not eligible for this recruitment.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for Agniveer (Apprentice) 01/2027 and 02/2027 Batch?
faq.answer: For the 01/2027 Batch, candidates must be born between 01 December 2004 and 31 May 2009 (both dates inclusive). For the 02/2027 Batch, candidates must be born between 01 May 2005 and 31 October 2009 (both dates inclusive). Candidates born between 01 Dec 2004 – 31 Oct 2009 are eligible to appear in INET 2/2026.

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification required for Agniveer (Apprentice) 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed Matriculation (Class 10th) with a minimum of 50% aggregate marks from a recognised Board, and must have completed a 3-year Diploma course from an AICTE-recognised Polytechnic Institute with a minimum of 50% marks in aggregate in any of the eligible Engineering or Electrical diploma trades.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary and Seva Nidhi package for Agniveer (Apprentice)?
faq.answer: Agniveers receive a monthly in-hand amount of ₹21,000 in Year 1, increasing to ₹28,000 in Year 4. Additionally, 30% of the monthly package is contributed to the Agniveer Corpus Fund, with a matching contribution by the Government of India. On completion of the 4-year engagement, Agniveers receive a one-time Seva Nidhi of ₹10.04 Lakhs. There is no gratuity or pension entitlement.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process consists of: Stage I – INET (Indian Navy Entrance Test, computer-based, 100 questions, 1 hour); followed by Stage II comprising Physical Fitness Test (PFT), Written Examination, and Recruitment Medical Examination; and finally a Pre-Enrolment Medical Examination at INS Chilka before induction. All stages are mandatory and qualifying.

faq.question: Q7. Is there negative marking in the Agniveer INET examination?
faq.answer: Yes. There is a penalty of 0.25 marks (one-fourth) deducted for every wrong answer in the INET examination. Candidates must pass in all sections as well as in aggregate. The Indian Navy reserves the right to determine the pass marks in each section and in aggregate.

faq.question: Q8. What is the application fee for Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) 2026?
faq.answer: The examination fee for Stage I – INET is ₹550/- plus 18% GST, payable online via net banking, credit card, debit card, or UPI. There is no separate fee for the Stage II process. Fee once paid is non-refundable under any circumstances. Sponsor candidates and NCC 'C' Certificate holders are exempted from Stage I – INET and are directly issued call-up letters for Stage II.

faq.question: Q9. Can an Agniveer be permanently enrolled in the Indian Navy after the 4-year tenure?
faq.answer: On completion of the 4-year engagement, up to 25% of each specific batch of Agniveers may be offered an opportunity to apply for permanent enrolment in the Indian Navy as Artificers (Sailors, Regular Cadre) on completion of induction training. Selection is based on objective criteria including performance during the 4-year engagement period and is entirely at the discretion of the Indian Navy. Agniveers have no right to claim permanent enrolment.

faq.question: Q10. What are the physical and medical standards for Agniveer (Apprentice) 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum height standard is 157 cms. For the Physical Fitness Test, male candidates must complete a 1.6 km run within 6 minutes 30 seconds, 20 squats, 15 push-ups, and 15 bent knee sit-ups. Visual standards require uncorrected vision of 6/12, 6/12 and corrected vision of 6/6, 6/6 with Colour Perception CP PASS. Permanent body tattoos are only permitted on the inner face of forearms and back of the hand; tattoos on any other part of the body will lead to disqualification. Candidates with gender reassignment surgery will be declared UNFIT.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for Agniveer (Apprentice) 01/2027 and 02/2027 Batch is 05 July 2026 (17:00 hrs). The correction window closes on 06 July 2026. Candidates are advised to apply well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues.

faq.answer: No. Only unmarried male candidates are eligible to apply for Agniveer (Apprentice) 01/2027 and 02/2027 Batch. Female candidates are not eligible for this recruitment.

faq.answer: For the 01/2027 Batch, candidates must be born between 01 December 2004 and 31 May 2009 (both dates inclusive).
